6451604ef8a47edd20cf9e83.question: One sculpture by this artist depicts a sad-looking figure with his knees near his chest, while another of his works depicts a reclining pregnant woman with her hand on her stomach. In addition to Elemental and Genesis, he also depicted a winged-man standing over a figure with horns in St. Michael and the Devil, which is located at the Coventry Cathedral with his sculpture Ecce Homo. This author of Let There Be Sculpture caused controversy with his two sculptures representing the life cycle entitled Day and Night; those works were commissioned by architect Charles Holden, who had earlier commissioned this man to create eighteen sculptures for the headquarters of the British Medical Association on the Strand. He may be best known for a sculpture of a sphinx on the tomb of Oscar Wilde or for a sculpture influenced by the Vorticism movement that represents a bleak future dominated by machinery. For 10 points, name this 20th century American-born British sculptor who created the Rock Drill.
